data.body:
PASS-1 review (T5c Security overlay) — PASS, no findings.

The plan adds a real security surface (public Gerrit 443/29418, ACME 80, webhook receiver, AWS IAM role, deploy key, bot token, SSM secrets) and handles all four T5c dimensions soundly:

- **Access levels / authz boundary:** endpoints declared (nginx 443 / ACME 80 / Gerrit SSH 29418; receiver loopback-bound + not in the SG). Inbound webhook auth explicit (ADR-0014: network-ACL primary via private compose net + URL token + receiver-side token/source validation; honest about `webhooks` having no HMAC). The gate-critical authz boundary is locked: `label-LLM-Review` votable ONLY by Service Users + Administrators (project.config), so a developer cannot self-vote past the gate. No inbound 22 (admin via SSM Session Manager).
- **Encryption:** TLS on 443; SSM SecureString (KMS) at rest; ed25519 deploy key; `replicatePermissions=false` keeps refs/meta/config (and the embedded URL token) off the GitHub mirror.
- **Least privilege:** IAM scoped to `arn:.../parameter/rebar/prod/*` (no account-wide wildcard); `kms:Decrypt` constrained by `kms:ViaService`; per-repo write-only deploy key as the sole ruleset bypass (`actor_id:null`); S2/S4a add scoped inline policies rather than re-declaring the role. The two `resources=["*"]` statements are either condition-bounded (kms) or the unavoidable CloudWatch PutMetricData/logs actions — not a least-privilege gap.
- **Secret lifecycle:** SSM SecureString source, `.env` at chmod 600, fail-fast on empty/None/CHANGEME, "never echoed", rotatable single token, git-ignored/never committed.

No grounded T5c defects.
